title: Data Ethics and Society
description: This course introduces ethical legal and societal questions around data-driven systems and artificial intelligence.

title: Sustainable Urban Mobility

title: Public Speaking and Rhetoric
description: The course trains presentation skills argumentation and confident communication in academic and professional contexts.

title: Entrepreneurship Basics
description: Introduction to business model design startup thinking and entrepreneurial decision-making.

title: Climate Change and Society
description: This course discusses climate change mitigation adaptation and the social consequences of environmental transformation.

title: Applied Machine Learning
description: Students build machine learning models for classification prediction and decision support with practical datasets.

title: Intercultural Communication
description: The course explores communication across cultures conflict styles and collaboration in international study and work settings.

title: Environmental Policy and Governance
description: Students analyze environmental policy instruments governance structures and stakeholder participation in sustainability transitions.

title: Digital Business Models
description: This course covers platform economics digital value creation and business models for technology-driven markets.

title: Design Thinking for Social Innovation
description: Students apply design thinking methods to social innovation challenges with user research prototyping and testing.

title: Psychology of Learning
description: The course introduces motivation cognition memory and evidence-based learning strategies for educational contexts.

title: Project Management in Practice

title: Health Promotion and Wellbeing
description: The course discusses stress prevention mental health health literacy and wellbeing in university and workplace settings.

title: Climate Data Analysis
description: Students work with environmental datasets to analyze climate trends uncertainty and impacts using statistical methods.

description: Students explore how artificial intelligence and optimization can support energy efficiency resource planning and climate action.

title: Urban Food Systems
description: The course examines food supply chains urban agriculture nutrition and sustainable city planning.

title: Negotiation and Conflict Management
description: Students practice negotiation strategies mediation basics and conflict resolution for organizational settings.

title: Human-Computer Interaction
description: The course covers user-centered design usability evaluation interaction principles and accessible digital interfaces.

title: Responsible Innovation
description: Students discuss ethical reflection stakeholder inclusion and societal impacts in research and innovation processes.

title: Introduction to Circular Economy

description: The course introduces heuristics biases decision-making experiments and applications in markets and public policy.

title: Ethics of Climate Intervention

title: Social Entrepreneurship

title: Environmental Psychology
description: The course explores human behavior environmental perception behavior change and sustainable lifestyles.

title: Smart Cities and Urban Analytics
description: Students analyze urban data sensors mobility systems and digital services for smart and sustainable cities.

title: Academic Writing in English
description: The course supports students in structuring arguments citing sources and writing clear academic texts in English.

title: Technology and Employment Law
description: Students learn how digital work platforms automation and remote work interact with employment law and regulation.

title: Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services
description: This course introduces biodiversity assessment ecosystem services conservation strategies and environmental trade-offs.

title: Creativity and Innovation Methods
description: Students use creative problem-solving methods ideation techniques and prototyping for interdisciplinary innovation.

description: Students learn survey analysis regression interpretation and reproducible workflows for social and economic data.
